title: gagea saxatilis var. corsica
intro: Gagea saxatilis var. corsica, also known as Corsican Gagea or Corsican Star of Bethlehem, is a plant species belonging to the Liliaceae family. It is a perennial plant with white or pale yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is said to offer various health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in respiratory issues. Traditionally, Gagea saxatilis var. corsica has been used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
